title: crataegus rotundifolia var. aboriginum
intro: Crataegus rotundifolia var. aboriginum, also known as the American hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its thorny branches and showy white flowers. The medicinal properties of C. rotundifolia var. aboriginum lie in its heart-shaped leaves, which contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health. The health benefits of this plant include reducing blood pressure, improving lipid profiles, and preventing or managing conditions such as atherosclerosis, high blood pressure, and angina. Herbal preparations of C. rotundifolia var. aboriginum include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to support heart health and overall well-being.
